titleStmt.title: Letter from A. Ross to Augustine Birrell, 30 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: A Ross

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from A. Ross to Augustine Birrell (1850–1933), the chief secretary for Ireland. In the letter Ross is asking Birrell to help put him in contact with his sister Miss Ross who works at Dublin Castle. Miss Ross worked as a cleaner in the Castle and according to the letter has no relations in Ireland.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/7557/2

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
A.u.s.,
This only came today.
Please let writer know
if Miss Ross is all right & oblige.
A.P.M. 159, Victoria Road,
New Brighton,
Cheshire, 30 April 1916. Dear Sir, I trust you will pardon me
for troubling you at such a busy and
anxious time as I am utterly at a
loss to know what else to do under
existing circumstances to allay the
anxiety of myself and others here
in regard to my sister (who has no
relatives in Ireland) Miss Ross,
Housekeeper, State Apartments, Dublin
Castle, but whose present address is
3, Upper Castle Yard. If you will kindly cause enquiries to
be made regarding her & have the
result communicated to me you
will confer a great favor on Chief Commr.
Please have
inquiry made and
communicate result to the
writer.
